title: Verify rational acyclicity H_*(\widetilde M;Q)=H_*(pt;Q) of the constructed cover

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: The constructed universal cover must satisfy $H_*(\widetilde M;\mathbb{Q})\cong H_*(\mathrm{pt};\mathbb{Q})$. Prove this via a transfer + Mayer–Vietoris computation comparing $\widetilde M$ to the contractible $X$, keeping $\mathbb{Q}$- and $\mathbb{Z}/2$-homology strictly separate: the cover is NOT $\mathbb{Z}/2$-acyclic (forced by Smith) yet must be $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic. The bookkeeping across the resolved strata is the delicate part.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved (conditionally on the construction inputs).** I wrote a self-contained note (`solution.tex`) discharging q7-7: a transfer + Mayer–Vietoris proof that the constructed cover $\widetilde M$ satisfies $H_*(\widetilde M;\mathbb{Q})\cong H_*(\mathrm{pt};\mathbb{Q})$, with $\mathbb{Q}$ and $\mathbb{Z}/2$ coefficients kept strictly separate.

**What is proved (gap-free).**
1. *Rational transfer (Lemma).* For a finite group $F$ acting freely on a finite-dim space $W$, $H^*(W/F;\mathbb{Q})\cong H^*(W;\mathbb{Q})^F$ (Cartan–Leray transfer; $|F|$ invertible / Maschke). Hence $W$ $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic $\Rightarrow$ $W/F$ $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic. This is the entire rational mechanism and it *fails* over $\mathbb{Z}/2$ (noted explicitly), which is precisely why mod-2 homology survives.
2. *Local rational equivalence (Lemma).* Each resolved tube piece $R_S\to N_S$ is a $\mathbb{Q}$-homology isomorphism, compatibly on boundaries/intersections (naturality of retractions + transfer over the stratum poset).
3. *Global comparison.* A single Mayer–Vietoris ladder comparing $\widetilde M = X^\circ\cup_{\partial N} R$ with $X = X^\circ\cup_{\partial N} N$. The maps over $H_*(\partial N)$ and over the $X^\circ$ summand are identities; $(\Phi|_R)_*:H_*(R;\mathbb{Q})\to H_*(N;\mathbb{Q})$ is an iso (map of Mayer–Vietoris spectral sequences, iso on $E^1$ by Lemma 2; equivalently induction over strata with the five lemma). The five lemma then gives $\Phi_*:H_*(\widetilde M;\mathbb{Q})\xrightarrow{\cong}H_*(X;\mathbb{Q})$. Since $X$ is contractible, $\widetilde M$ is $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic.
4. *Consistency section.* Explicitly checks no conflict with non-vanishing mod-2 homology: every iso used is rational; Smith needs $\mathbb{Z}/2$-acyclicity; compact Lefschetz fails ($\widetilde M$ noncompact, free cocompact deck action, compactly-supported $\chi=0$).

**Honest scope / caveat.** The proof is *conditional on* the construction inputs (I1)–(I4), which belong to issues q7-4, q7-5, q7-6 — in particular the key rational-control input (I3)/eq.(16): that the resolved tube structure map $R\to N$ is a *stratumwise* rational homology equivalence with the stated naturality. Given those inputs, the $H_*(\widetilde M;\mathbb{Q})=H_*(\mathrm{pt};\mathbb{Q})$ computation is complete and rigorous. The note states clearly which facts are quoted vs. proved here. I therefore consider q7-7's specific obligation (the transfer + Mayer–Vietoris verification, with $\mathbb{Q}$/$\mathbb{Z}_2$ separation) discharged; the remaining geometric content lives in the cited issues. Note: no prior `solution.tex` existed, so this is the first content for the file.
